sn20.1:1.2: that on one occasion the Blessed One was staying near Sāvatthī in Jeta's Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s monastery.
sn20.1:1.3: There he addressed the monks: “Monks!” “Yes, lord,” the monks responded to him. The Blessed One said,
sn20.1:1.4: “Monks, just as all the rafters in a ridge-roofed house go to the ridge-beam, converge on the ridge-beam, and are removed when the ridge-beam is removed,
sn20.1:1.5: in the same way, all unskillful qualities are rooted in ignorance, converge on ignorance, and are removed when ignorance is removed.
sn20.1:1.8: That’s how you should train yourselves.”
